skill.name: lark-note
skill.description: Retrieves Lark meeting note details for an explicit note_id, including display type, related document tokens, and unified transcript routing.
skill.summary: Look up Lark Note details and unified transcripts when a note_id is already known.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found the hits are markdown command examples, inline code spans, and relative documentation links. No prompt injection, malicious intent, encoded payload, or executable script was found in the reviewed files. The remaining risk is low because the skill intentionally routes users to lark-cli commands and can save transcript files locally.

low_findings.title: CLI Commands Are Documented for User-Directed Lark Access
low_findings.description: The skill includes lark-cli command examples for note detail and transcript retrieval. These are markdown instructions, not executable bundled code, but users should understand that running them accesses Lark meeting note data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines show explicit lark-cli usage and routing tables. There is no shell script, dynamic command construction, or hidden execution path in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Transcript Retrieval Writes Local Output Files
low_findings.description: The transcript reference states that unified transcript output is saved under a local notes directory and that overwrite requires an explicit flag. This is expected behavior but still represents local filesystem writes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write behavior is directly documented on the cited lines. The risk is limited because the path is scoped to a local notes directory and overwrite is opt-in.

low_findings.title: Static Traversal and Obfuscation Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported traversal hits come from relative documentation links to sibling Lark skills, and the obfuscation alert points at a Chinese description string. No encoded payload or filesystem traversal behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain human-readable description text and markdown links. I found no code that interprets user-controlled paths or decodes an opaque payload.

content.value_statement: Teams need a reliable way to inspect Lark meeting notes when they already have a note_id. This skill routes Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through the correct Lark note detail and transcript workflows.
actual_capabilities: Routes explicit note_id requests to Lark note detail lookup.
Identifies note display types such as normal, unknown, and unified.
Explains when to fetch note documents or verbatim transcript documents.
Directs unified transcript requests to the note transcript command.
Documents how transcript output is saved and when overwrite is allowed.
limitations: Requires an explicit note_id or a vc-node-id from a fetched document.
Does not search meetings, calendars, titles, or document backlinks.
Does not read Docx content directly; it routes that work to Lark document skills.
Requires lark-cli and valid Lark access outside the skill files.

use_cases.title: Check meeting note metadata
use_cases.description: A meeting owner can inspect the note display type and related document tokens for a known note_id.

use_cases.title: Route transcript retrieval
use_cases.description: An operations teammate can choose the correct transcript path for normal, unknown, or unified notes.

use_cases.title: Support Lark workspace workflows
use_cases.description: An IT support user can explain why a note request needs a note_id before transcript access is attempted.

prompt_templates.title: Look up a note
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a known note_id.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use lark-note to check this note_id and tell me the note display type and available document tokens: NOTE_ID.

prompt_templates.title: Choose transcript route
prompt_templates.scenario: User has detail output and needs the next step.
prompt_templates.prompt: Given this Lark note detail result, decide whether I should fetch a document transcript or use unified transcript retrieval.

prompt_templates.title: Handle vc-node-id
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a document fetch result with a vc-node-id.
prompt_templates.prompt: This document fetch result contains a vc-node-id. Treat it as the note_id and route the next Lark note detail request.

prompt_templates.title: Audit transcript workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of transcript handling and output behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Lark note workflow. Confirm the note_id source, transcript route, output file behavior, and any reason to stop.

output_examples.input: I have note_id abc123. What should I check first?
output_examples.output: First retrieve the note detail for abc123. Use the display type to decide whether to fetch a document transcript or unified transcript.

output_examples.input: The note display type is unified. How do I get the raw transcript?
output_examples.output: Use the unified transcript route for the same note_id. The transcript is saved locally under the notes output directory unless a conflict stops it.

output_examples.input: I only have the meeting title. Can this skill find the transcript?
output_examples.output: No. Start with document or meeting search in another Lark skill. Return to lark-note only after you have a note_id or vc-node-id.

best_practices: Start only when the user provides a note_id or a document result with vc-node-id.
Use note_display_type as the routing key before choosing a transcript source.
Ask before overwriting local transcript output files.
anti_patterns: Do not infer a note_id from a document token, title, backlink, or body text.
Do not treat every verbatim_doc_token as a unified transcript source.
Do not retry transcript retrieval when the detail result cannot identify a valid route.

faq.question: What input does this skill need?
faq.answer: It needs an explicit note_id or a vc-node-id found in a fetched Lark document.

faq.question: Can it search for meeting notes by title?
faq.answer: No. Use the appropriate Lark document, drive, meeting, or calendar workflow before using this skill.

faq.question: Does it read Docx content?
faq.answer: No. It routes Docx reading to Lark document skills after it identifies the correct document token.

faq.question: When should unified transcript retrieval be used?
faq.answer: Use it when note detail confirms the note_display_type is unified.

faq.question: What happens if the transcript output file already exists?
faq.answer: The documented workflow fails by default and requires explicit overwrite approval.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
